text: Ever wondered how genes stay balanced in a population? The Hardy Weinberg idea explains this balance using simple math and assumptions. It assumes no mutations, random mating, no migration, large populations, and no selection. In real life, these conditions are rarely met. Yet, it’s a powerful tool! Understanding Hardy Weinberg helps us grasp how evolution impacts genetic diversity. Curious about genetics? Hit like and follow for more science insights!
